name: Creole Pralines
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine sugars and cream in a heavy saucepan.
2. Stir over low heat until the sugar dissolves.
3. Increase heat and cook rapidly without stirring until the mixture registers 230-234°F on a candy thermometer (soft ball stage).
4. Stir in butter and pecans.
5. Cook until the mixture reaches 234°F again, then remove from heat.
6. Quickly drop spoonfuls of the mixture onto buttered wax paper.
7. Store in an airtight container.
"My husband is from the N'awleans area and this is his favorite candy. You just have to make sure you are making it on a rather dry day...it does not do well in high humidity."
